Founder Experience.question: Has the founder or co-founder had a successful exit via M&A or IPO?
Has the founder or co-founder previously been a successful CEO with notable achievements in the industry?

Industry Experience.question: What is the founder or co-founder's prior work experience?
options: Generic work experience (not relevant to the industry)
Experience as a product manager, in sales, technology, or roles specific to the industry
Experience in building a startup with successful product launches or scaling revenue
Experience as a C-suite executive (e.g., COO, CTO, CFO) with deep domain expertise

Education.question: What is the highest level of education attained by the founder or co-founder?
options: General degrees not specific to the industry
Elite/top-tier university degree relevant to the industry

Team Composition.question: How well-rounded is the founding team?
options: Solo founder or team covers few functional areas
Team of entrepreneurs with complementary skills
Complete management team with a well-rounded founding team
Co-founders or team members have previously worked together in a startup or company

Market Size.question: What is the estimated total addressable market (TAM) for the product or service?

Market Growth.question: What is the annual growth rate of the market?

Revenue Growth.question: Is the startup generating revenue with strong month-over-month or year-over-year growth?

Revenue.question: What is the current revenue status of the startup?
options: B. Some revenue generated, inconsistent growth
C. Some revenue generated, consistent moderate growth
D. Generating revenue but not consistently growing
E. Generating consistent and moderately growing revenue

Strategic Partnerships and Pilot Program.question: Does the startup have any strategic partnerships or pilot programmes?
options: A. No strategic partnerships or pilot programs
B. Some partnerships/pilot programs indicating initial market validation
C. Several partnerships/pilot programs indicating initial market validation
D. Multiple partnerships/pilot programs indicating strong market validation
E. Exclusive partnerships or numerous successful pilot programs

User Engagement / Organic Social Media G.question: What is the level of user engagement or organic social media growth for the startup?
options: A. Low engagement, minimal organic growth on socials
B. Moderate engagement, some organic growth on socials
C. Decent engagement, consistent organic growth on socials
D. High engagement, strong organic growth on socials
E. Very high engagement, key opinion leader on socials

Revenue Growth.question: What stage is the product/solution currently in?

Scalability.question: How scalable is the product/solution?
options: Moderately scalable with potential for significant growth
Highly scalable, can handle significant growth
Highly scalable, designed for extensive growth and additional use cases

Vitamin or Painkiller.question: How essential is the product/solution to the target market?
options: Vitamin, nice to have but not essential
Somewhat important, addresses some needs but not critical

Competitive Landscape.title: Strength of Competitors in the Marketplace

Revenue Growth.question: How would the competitive landscape be described?
options: A. Dominated by a single large player
B. Dominated by a few large players

Strength of Competitive Products.question: How would the strength of competitive products be rated?

Differentiation / Unique Selling Points.question: How differentiated is the product/solution from competitors?
options: A. Limited or no differentiation, easily copied, no Intellectual property
C. Moderately differentiated, some unique aspects
D. Unique and protected by specialized know-how / trade secrets
